events.title: Mystical Arts of Tibet
events.description: Observe an ancient ritual of creation and impermanence as Tibetan Buddhist monks from the Drepung Loseling Monastery create and then ritually destroy an intricate sand mandala painting. In keeping with centuries-old tradition the lamas begin by consecrating the site through chants, music, and mantra recitation. Over the days that follow, the lamas create a mandala by meticulously hand-placing millions of colored grains of sand on a wooden platform. Monday, April 6 – Friday, April 10 Opening ceremony: April 6, 12 p.m. Viewing: April 6, 7, and 9, 1–7 p.m.; April 8, 9 a.m. – 6 p.m. Closing ceremo

events.title: Opening Ceremony: Mystical Arts of Tibet
events.description: In keeping with centuries-old tradition, Tibetan Buddhist monks from the Drepung Loseling Monastery create and then ritually destroy an intricate sand mandala painting. Held prior to the creation of an intricate sand mandala painting, the opening ceremony honors dignitaries and consecrates the grounds of the mandala with sacred chants believed to generate energies conducive to world healing. Following the ceremony, the monks begin the intricate process of creating the mandala, placing millions of grains of sand by hand to form detailed and symbolic designs.

events.title: Supporting LGBTQIA+ Survivors of Violence
events.description: Be a green flag, support people impacted by violence. SASA - sexual assault awareness month. 413-545-0800 A presentation by CWC Staff. Open to all. Sponsored by CWC and the Stonewall Center.

events.title: SpACE+ Custom Playing Cards
events.description: Design your own playing cards and enjoy cupcakes for International Ace Day. The SpACE+ is a student group for ace and aro communities, but all are welcome to attend.

events.title: HR is Here for You
events.description: Looking for job opportunities at UMass Amherst? Need help with your application or have questions about the hiring process? Are you a new employee with questions about onboarding? UMass Amherst Human Resources is here to help! The UMass HR team offers monthly community office hours at UMass Downtown to provide application assistance and interview guidance for prospective employees and CORI verifications and onboarding support for new hires. No appointment necessary. Just stop by during UMass HR's community office hours!

events.title: 9th Annual Building Bridges Showcase
events.description: Building Bridges invites the campus community and beyond to attend the 9th annual Building Bridges 2026 showcase, taking place Tuesday, April 7 from noon to 7 p.m. at the Randolph W. Bromery Center for the Arts. The Showcase Event is an annual gathering where UMass staff and their creative talents take center stage. This year, the showcase features work from over 70 worker artists across campus. The showcase—curated and installed by participants in the Showcasing Worker Artists class, a core Building Bridges offering through the Partnership for Worker Education—will be on view from 12-7pm, wit

events.title: Data Analytics and Computational Social Science Information Session
events.description: Join the School of Public Policy for an info session on the Master of Data Analytics and Computational Social Science (DACSS) program. You will learn about admissions requirements for the program, hear about the coursework offered, and have the opportunity to ask any questions you may have.

events.title: Online & Hybrid MBA Virtual Information Session
events.description: Join us for an interactive overview of Isenberg’s Online and Hybrid MBA programs. Connect with a faculty member, a current student, and members of our support team to learn what makes our program exceptional. This session is your opportunity to ask questions about the student experience, online learning, career resources, curriculum, and the many tools available to help you succeed. Bonus: One lucky registrant will receive an application fee waiver! Register today and take the next step toward your MBA.

events.title: BPQC Meeting: Bi/Pan Stigma in Queer Spaces
events.description: The Bi/Pan/Queer Collective (BPQC), a social group for bi, pan, queer, and sexually fluid students, will be discussing bi/pan stigma in queer spaces at its next meeting. Open to all students.

events.title: Libraries' Page to Screen Club
events.description: Drop in to the Libraries' multimedia book club, where we read a book and then watch the screen adaptation! We meet for several weeks to talk about the book, then watch the screen adaptation together and compare. All sessions are 7-8:30 p.m. except for screenings, which are 7-9:30 p.m. The breakdown for what pages/sections to read will be determined at a later planning session. Little Women, Louisa May Alcott February 3 February 10 February 17 February 24 March 3 - Screening Stories of Your Life and Others, Ted Chiang March 10 March 17 - No meeting; spring vacation March 24 - Screening Jurassic

events.title: Lecture: Why Do We Need Each Other?
events.description: In keeping with centuries-old tradition, from April 6–10, Tibetan Buddhist monks from the Drepung Loseling Monastery create and then ritually destroy an intricate sand mandala painting. On April 8, one of the monks delivers a special lecture on belonging in an increasingly globalized world. Why Do We Need Each Other? Lecture by Gonsa Rinpoche with translation by Geshe Tsewang Punchok In a changing reality, shaped particularly by social media and increasing globalization, we have become much closer and much more dependent on each other. These changes are neither inherently positive nor negative

events.title: 2026 Elaine Marieb Center Nursing and Engineering Innovation Symposium
events.description: Theme: Building the Next Generation of Healthcare Innovators This year’s NEIS brings together nurses, engineers, students, and industry leaders to explore how interdisciplinary collaboration drives the future of healthcare innovation. Through keynotes, panels, and interactive sessions, participants will share insights, research, and strategies to empower the next generation of innovators transforming patient care.
